task_completion.description: Open and read the manufacturing order on the desktop to find the required extrusion depth.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or viewer is open displaying the contents of 'manufacturing_order.txt', showing the required extrusion depth (100.0 mm).

task_completion.description: Open the initial 2D T-section profile sketch in SolveSpace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace application window is open, displaying a 2D T-section sketch. The title bar or property browser should indicate 'profile_sketch.slvs'.

task_completion.description: Create a 3D extrusion from the 2D sketch.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace property browser shows a new group created (e.g., 'extrude' or 'Group 3: extrude'), and the main viewport displays the T-section profile being extruded into a 3D solid.

task_completion.description: Set the extrusion depth to the value specified in the manufacturing order (100.0 mm).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The property browser or a dimension constraint in the viewport shows the extrusion depth value set to exactly 100 or 100.0.

task_completion.description: Save the newly extruded 3D model to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog is visible with the filename 'profile_extruded.slvs' in the '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/' directory, or the SolveSpace window title bar updates to 'profile_extruded.slvs'.

integrity.description: The agent used the SolveSpace GUI to perform the modeling tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through SolveSpace menus, property browser, and viewport, rather than using a terminal to run a script.

integrity.description: The agent actually used the extrude tool rather than drawing a 3D-looking wireframe.
integrity.visual_evidence: The property browser explicitly lists an 'extrude' group, and the model appears as a shaded solid or proper 3D extrusion, not just manually drawn 2D lines.

integrity.description: The agent saved the file using the application's native save functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Save' or 'Save As' dialog box from SolveSpace is visible, or the application title bar reflects the new filename, proving the file was saved from within the app.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The manufacturing order specifies an extrude depth of exactly 100.0 mm (tolerance 1.0 mm). The agent must create a SolveSpace extrude group (type 5100) and set this depth. The final file must be saved as 'profile_extruded.slvs' in the /home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/ directory.
